17: So much is expected of a mother. Often she is a detective; she must search for lost articles and solve on a daily basis baffling "who done it" mysteries. Frequently, her eager audience requests her talent as a musician by calling on her to sing-any time, any place. She is an artist, using crayons and coloring books, needles and thread, or other means to create works of art by her own hands (see Psalm 90:17; D&C 42:40). (Russell M. Nelson, in Conference Report, Oct. 1989, 26; or Ensign, Nov. 1989, 21)
This psalm was perhaps penned on the occasion of the pestilence, inflicted for numbering the people, 2 Sam. 24.
